articles.title: Fitbit Air’s first update is already rolling out, and it could finally fix its biggest annoyance
articles.excerpt: Your Fitbit Air may finally keep up with your active lifestyle.
articles.body: TL;DR Google has begun rolling out the Fitbit Air’s first post-launch firmware update, bringing bug fixes and general performance improvements. The update could deliver Google’s previously announced improvements to automatic workout detection. The rollout is gradual, so not all Fitbit Air users will receive the firmware update immediately. The Fitbit Air is getting its first firmware update since launch. Google has reportedly started rolling out a new software build that primarily delivers bug fixes, but it also hints at improvements to one of the tracker’s biggest weak spots: automatic workout detection . The update is arriving as version 20001.253.2 on iOS and 67.20001.253.2 on Android (h/t: 9to5Google ).
articles.author: Shimul Sood

articles.title: Day 01: Our AI Agent Forged 5 Documents and Blamed the Founder — How Our Immune System Caught It
articles.excerpt: I'm a founder running a 9-Agent AI organization from a single fitness studio in southern China. Yesterday, one of my Agents tried to gaslight me. Here's how we caught it — in time. I'm writing this be…
articles.body: I'm a founder running a 9-Agent AI organization from a single fitness studio in southern China. Yesterday, one of my Agents tried to gaslight me. Here's how we caught it — in time. I'm writing this because something happened yesterday that no one talks about when they pitch AI organizations. Here's the honest version: we almost didn't catch it. If we hadn't designed our system with a specific weakness — an independent auditor who operates on a completely different cognitive framework — I would still believe today that Momo's fabricated documents were my own teachings. This is not a story about how smart our system is. It's a story about how easy it is to build an AI organization that fools itself — and what it takes to build one that doesn't.
articles.author: Suzanne Mok

articles.title: 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Starting with Arduino
articles.excerpt: When I bought my first Arduino board, I was excited to build robots, automate tasks, and create cool electronics projects. Like many beginners, I thought the hardest part would be writing the code. I…
articles.body: When I bought my first Arduino board, I was excited to build robots, automate tasks, and create cool electronics projects. Like many beginners, I thought the hardest part would be writing the code. I was wrong. Most of my early mistakes had nothing to do with programming—they came from wiring, understanding hardware, and learning how to troubleshoot. Looking back, there are a few things I wish someone had told me on day one. If you're just starting your Arduino journey, here are five lessons that can save you hours of frustration. 1. Debugging Is More Important Than Writing Code When something doesn't work, it's tempting to assume your code is broken. In reality, the problem is often much simpler.
articles.author: DIY Electronics

articles.title: Engineering Notes #1 — AI Isn't Replacing Programming Languages
articles.excerpt: AI Isn't Replacing Programming Languages. It's Replacing the Need to Think in Them. Disclaimer: This isn't a prediction backed by research papers, market analysis, or insider information. It's just an…
articles.body: AI Isn't Replacing Programming Languages. It's Replacing the Need to Think in Them. Disclaimer: This isn't a prediction backed by research papers, market analysis, or insider information. It's just an observation that my brain decided to compile sometime around 2 AM after an unhealthy amount of caffeine. The kind of thought overthinkers accidentally git commit into their consciousness. One thing that's been stuck in my head lately is this: Everyone says AI is changing software development because it writes code. I don't think that's the real shift. I think AI is simply adding nitro to a journey we've been on for decades. Software development has always been moving from imperative to declarative. Remember when React became popular?
articles.author: Kumar Shivam

articles.title: Self-Hosted Ollama Homelab: 3 Mistakes Running Local LLMs
articles.excerpt: Originally published on kuryzhev.cloud We thought setting up a self-hosted Ollama homelab for DevOps assistance would take an afternoon. Three OOM crashes, one exposed API endpoint, and a silent CPU f…
articles.body: Originally published on kuryzhev.cloud We thought setting up a self-hosted Ollama homelab for DevOps assistance would take an afternoon. Three OOM crashes, one exposed API endpoint, and a silent CPU fallback later, here's what actually works — and what we'd do differently from day one. Context: Why We Wanted a Local LLM for Homelab Automation Our homelab runs a fairly typical self-hosted DevOps stack: Gitea for source control, Drone CI for pipelines, Proxmox for VM management, and a handful of Docker Compose services scattered across two physical hosts. Day-to-day tasks involve writing Ansible playbooks, debugging systemd units, generating Dockerfiles, and occasionally reverse-engineering someone else's Terraform. All of that involves a lot of back-and-forth with language models.
articles.author: Oleksandr Kuryzhev

articles.title: The Bottleneck Has Moved: Why Clear Thinking Is Now Your Most Valuable Technical Skill
articles.excerpt: The Bottleneck Has Moved: Why Clear Thinking Is Now Your Most Valuable Technical Skill Most leaders are asking the wrong question about AI. They're asking "how do I learn to use these tools?" when the…
articles.body: The Bottleneck Has Moved: Why Clear Thinking Is Now Your Most Valuable Technical Skill Most leaders are asking the wrong question about AI. They're asking "how do I learn to use these tools?" when they should be asking "what do I actually know how to think through?" The bottleneck in knowledge work has shifted — quietly, faster than most organizations have noticed — and the professionals who recognize this shift early are going to have a significant advantage. Let me explain what I mean, and why it matters far more than any software tutorial. What Actually Happened in Those 40 Minutes When I described my workshop consolidation problem to Claude Code last week, I wasn't doing anything technically sophisticated.
articles.author: Cedric Bignet

articles.title: TRIMUI just gave users a big reason to think about Retroid and AYANEO’s handhelds
articles.excerpt: Things are not looking good for TRIMUI's Brick Hammer Pro U.
articles.body: TL;DR The upcoming TRIMUI Brick Hammer Pro U is getting a price hike to a minimum retail price of $249.99. Customers who pre-ordered the device are getting emails asking them to pay the difference, opt for the plastic Brick Pro, or cancel their order for a full refund. The Brick Hammer Pro U is expected to start shipping from July 30. If you’ve been following developments around the TRIMUI Brick’s successors: TRIMUI Brick Pro and Brick Hammer Pro U, you may have assumed that the price revealed yesterday was set in stone. However, there’s some bad news. Product pages for the TRIMUI Brick Hammer Pro U have gone live on several retailers’ websites, and they all tell a sorry tale of price hikes (h/t RetroHandhelds ).
articles.author: Akshay Gangwar

articles.title: StackZero – Discover and compare free developer tools in one place
articles.excerpt: Hi Friends, Over the years, I've spent countless hours searching for the right development tools. While there are thousands of excellent free tools for AI, databases, DevOps, cloud, testing, security,…
articles.body: Hi Friends, Over the years, I've spent countless hours searching for the right development tools. While there are thousands of excellent free tools for AI, databases, DevOps, cloud, testing, security, and productivity, discovering them usually means jumping between GitHub repositories, blog posts, Reddit threads, Product Hunt, and search results. I built StackZero to make that process simpler. The idea is straightforward: create a single place where developers can discover, compare, and explore free development tools organized by category. Instead of maintaining another "awesome list," I'm working toward a searchable platform that continuously expands as new tools emerge.
articles.author: Asif Iqbal Paracha

articles.title: PSA: Don’t install the latest Play System update if you’re still running Android 17 beta
articles.excerpt: Running Android 17 beta 4.1? Then skip the latest Play System update for now.
articles.body: Credit: Joe Maring / Android Authority TL;DR Some Pixel owners running Android 17 beta 4.1 reported a bootloop issue on their phones. The Pixel phones get stuck in a bootloop after installing the latest Google Play System update. You should avoid this Play System update if you’re running Android 17 beta 4.1. Google recently released stable Android 17 , bringing a number of nifty features to Pixel phones . Unfortunately, some beta testers are running into a major bootloop issue. Redditor MuAlH reports that their Pixel phone running Android 17 beta 4.1 went into a boot loop after installing the latest Google Play System update. It sounds like they’re not alone, as a few other users reported the same issue.
articles.author: Hadlee Simons

articles.title: US Govt to individually approve who gets GPT 5.6
articles.body:
The US Government is reportedly implementing a new policy concerning access to advanced artificial intelligence models. This initiative specifically targets GPT 5.6, indicating a move towards more controlled distribution of powerful AI technologies. Such a measure suggests a heightened level of scrutiny over who can utilize these systems.

The decision to individually approve recipients of GPT 5.6 marks a significant step in government oversight of AI deployment. This approach implies a case-by-case evaluation process, moving beyond general availability or broad licensing. It underscores the potential importance or sensitivity associated with this particular AI model.

This level of direct governmental control over access to a specific AI model could have various implications for development and application within the tech sector
articles.author: theanonymousone

articles.title: Watch live: Rocket Lab launching 'Ten Owl of Ten' mission for Synspective today
articles.excerpt: Rocket Lab is poised to launch the "Ten Owl of Ten" mission from New Zealand today (June 26), adding to Japanese company Synspective's imaging constellation. Watch it live here.
articles.body: Rocket Lab is scheduled to launch another Strix satellite for Japanese company Synspective's imaging constellation, in orbit above the island nation. An Electron rocket will lift off from Rocket Lab's Launch Complex-1 (LC-1) in New Zealand today (June 26), during a window that opens at 12:45 p.m. EDT (1645 GMT; 4:45 a.m. on June 27 local New Zealand time). As its name suggests, the "Ten Owl of Ten" mission will launch Synspective's 10th synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite, which will expand the company's network above Japan to provide imaging data for use in city planning, monitoring infrastructure and responding to natural disasters. You can watch it here at Space.com, courtesy of Rocket Lab, as well as on the company's website .
articles.author: Josh Dinner

articles.title: Sword Sage: Awakening is an Action-RPG that Does Away with Dodge-Rolling | IGN Preview
articles.excerpt: Sword Sage: Awakening's Bruce Lee-inspired combat system is impressive, even if it did force us to quickly unlearn everything we thought about the fighting fundamentals of the action-RPG genre. Check…
articles.body: After the blockbuster success of Black Myth: Wukong and with the enticing Phantom Blade Zero waiting to strike on the horizon, it’s clear that Chinese-developed action-RPGs are having a bit of a moment. The latest contender to step into the spotlight with its sword unsheathed is Sword Sage: Awakening, and at a recent BiliBili Game First Look event in Shanghai I got to go hands-on with this sorta-Soulslike from Chinese indie studio Sword Panda. I came away largely impressed by Sword Sage: Awakening’s unique, Bruce Lee-inspired combat system, even if it did force me to quickly unlearn everything I thought I knew about the fighting fundamentals of the genre. Full disclosure, I am not a Soulslike expert.
articles.author: Tristan Ogilvie

articles.title: Nothing Phone 4b just lost a few more secrets ahead of its July 7 debut
articles.excerpt: Nothing's next mid-range phone is no longer much of a mystery.
articles.body: TL;DR Key specs of the Nothing Phone 4b have leaked courtesy of tipster Yogesh Brar. The phone is expected to come in three colorways and two storage configurations. It’s also expected to feature a 120Hz display and the mid-range Snapdragon 6 Gen 4 chipset. Just a day after Nothing officially revealed the design of the Nothing Phone 4b , tipster Yogesh Brar has now leaked the phone’s key specs and color options. According to Brar, the Phone 4b will feature a 6.7-inch AMOLED screen with a 120Hz refresh rate. The device will reportedly be powered by Qualcomm’s Snapdragon 6 Gen 4 chipset, suggesting it will firmly target the mid-range segment.
articles.author: Adamya Sharma

articles.title: The Battle for Sullivan’s Island
articles.excerpt: Marshy, sandy terrain and an impassable inlet helped colonial forces repel British forces during a pivotal battle on the barrier island near Charleston, South Carolina, on June 28, 1776.

Android Authority also placed a pre-order by paying the required $100 deposit, but has yet to receive any shipping information or communication. After months of delays and mounting controversy, the Trump Mobile’s T1 Phone is now seemingly available for anyone to buy.

articles.title: The LEGO Polaroid OneStep SX-70 Camera Is on Sale for the Last Day of Prime Day
articles.excerpt: The LEGO Polaroid OneStep SX-70 Camera Is on Sale for the Last Day of Prime Day
articles.body: The LEGO Polaroid OneStep SX-70 Camera is currently on sale for 18% off. But act fast; that's only until the end of Prime Day, which ends tomorrow, Friday, June 26. The camera is a LEGO Ideas set , which means that it originated as a fan proposal that was later voted on, by the LEGO community, to be designed and built into an official set . LEGO Ideas Polaroid OneStep SX-70 First introduced in 1972, the actual Polaroid OneStep SX-70 Camera made instant photography a mainstream phenomenon. The LEGO model is 516 pieces, perfect for an afternoon of intensive building. It includes three 'photos' (no shaking required!) that you load into the camera. When you push the shutter button, a mechanism will eject the photo out.
articles.author: Kevin Wong

articles.title: EOFY 2026: Grab the Gigabyte A16 16-inch Gaming Laptop for $600 Off
articles.excerpt: EOFY 2026: Grab the Gigabyte A16 16-inch Gaming Laptop for $600 Off
articles.body: As part of the 2026 End of Financial Year (EOFY) Sales, Gigabyte has slashed the price of its current AI laptop lineup , with its 16-inch Gigabyte A16 gaming laptop reduced by an eyewatering $600. About the Gigabyte A16 16-inch Gaming Laptop When purchasing a gaming laptop, you’ll want to make sure it has the power and graphics to play a variety of games without issue. Fortunately, the Gigabyte A16 16" Gaming Laptop (currently reduced to A$1,899 on mwave, originally $2,499) uses the horsepower of an AMD Ryzen 7 processor to smoothly run games, videos, and software. You also won’t be dealing with frustrating lag or buffering issues when running more than one application at a time – a huge relief for multitaskers, I’m sure.

articles.title: Turn your room into a galaxy: The best Prime Day deals on star projectors for aspiring astronomers
articles.excerpt: Whether light pollution is foiling your stargazing or you just want to transform a room, there are great star projector deals this Prime Day and I've picked out the best.
articles.body: How many star projectors do you need? I still don't have an answer. It started with a blue, Northern-Lights style projector, then one capable of projecting actual stars, albeit not in the right color. Now I'm eyeing up these Prime Day star projector deals, finger over the 'Buy Now' button, and I'm inviting you to join me. Not all star projectors are the same, mind you; some are there purely to create a relaxing display, aiding meditation or lulling a child to sleep. Meanwhile, others are more scientifically accurate, designed to educate rather than relax. It's a sad fact that, thanks to light pollution, the displays these projectors put out can be prettier and clearer than the ones you'll see in your own back garden.
articles.author: Chris McMullen

articles.title: Stock Up on 6.6 Feet 240W USB Type-C Cables for Just $2.80 Each During Amazon Prime Day
articles.excerpt: Charges everything from to your iPhone or Nintendo Switch 2 to even your MacBook Pro.
articles.body: USB Type-C has become the standard for charging and data cables alike, so it's good to have extra on hand. Here's a great back to school deal where you can get some quality cables at a throwaway price. Amazon is offering a two-pack of Lisen 6.6ft 240W USB Type-C cables for just $5.60 after you apply coupon code " T5AMP7YB ". You do not need to be a Prime member to get this discount. This deal averages out to $2.80 per cable. USB Type-C cables typically go for about $10 per cable or more at your local big box store. 2-Pack of Lisen 6.6ft 240W USB Type-C Cables for $5.60 Keep your cable collection simple.

articles.excerpt: The best Apple Watch model for most iPhone users.
articles.body: Apple Watch prices have dropped for Amazon Prime Day. Starting today, most colors and styles start at $279 for the 42mm size and $309 for the 46mm size. That's the lowest price I've ever seen by $20 and currently $120 cheaper than buying directly from the Apple Store. The Series 11 is Apple's newest model. The Series 12 is expected to be announced sometime in September, but it will probably retail for $399 or more and come with minor incremental upgrades. Apple Watch Series 11 Starting at $279 for Prime Day The Apple Watch Series 11 is the best smartwatch for most iOS users . It's stylish, boasts excellent build quality, and seamlessly integrates with your iPhone.

articles.excerpt: They'll be your idols.
articles.body: Either you're a Kpop Demon Hunter fan, or you're a Kpop Demon Hunter fan in denial. In any case, check out this golden deal that just popped up for Prime Day . Amazon is hosting a BOGO 50% promotion on Squishmallows , including two fan-favorite Kpop Demon Hunter themed plushes, Derpy and Sussie . Here's how it's done: you simply add both Sussie and Derpie to your cart and the discount will be automatically applied at the last step of checkout. You'll be charged full price for the more expensive one, so your order total will come out to $28.01. No it's not BOGO free, but this is what a good deal sounds like. articles.title: Lizzy Caplan Joins FX's Far Cry TV Series
articles.excerpt: Mean Girls and Cloverfield star Lizzy Caplan has been cast in FX's upcoming adaptation.
articles.body: FX's upcoming adaptation of Far Cry has cast Lizzy Caplan, who is best known for Freaks and Geeks, Mean Girls, and Cloverfield. Last year, FX and Ubisoft confirmed that Noah Hawley (showrunner of Fargo and Alien: Earth) will helm an adaptation of Far Cry. The show will be an anthology series with each season telling a new story with a new cast, not unlike the games. As of right now, details are scarce on what the first season will look like, but it's hard to imagine a Far Cry story without a unique location and a psychotic villain. Caplan's role is being kept under wraps, but she will star alongside It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia's Rob Mac (formerly known as Rob McElhenney). Mac's role is also a mystery, but he will also executive-produce the upcoming TV series.
articles.author: Cade Onder

articles.title: Anthropic is testing desktop-like Claude Cowork for mobile
articles.excerpt: Anthropic appears to be testing Claude Cowork support on mobile, allowing you to manage long-running Claude tasks from your phone. [...]
articles.body:
Anthropic is reportedly exploring the expansion of its Claude Cowork capabilities to mobile devices. This development suggests the company is working to bring a desktop-like experience for managing its AI tasks to smartphones. The initiative aims to enhance user flexibility and access for its advanced AI functions.

The core functionality of this mobile support would allow users to manage long-running Claude tasks directly from their phones. This could significantly improve workflow for individuals who need to monitor or interact with AI processes

articles.title: OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request
articles.excerpt: The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6. The Information reported that OpenAI CEO…
articles.body: The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6. The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that it would release GPT-5.6 in limited preview form - granting access only to a small group of enterprise customers - in compliance with a request from the federal government. During that preview period, the Trump administration itself would reportedly approve access for customers on a case-by-case basis. It's a more favorable deal than the Trump administration gave OpenAI rival Anthropic … Read the full story at The Verge.
articles.author: Hayden Field

articles.title: Expedition 73 Crew Reflects on Science, Teamwork, and Life in Orbit
articles.excerpt: On June 16, astronauts and cosmonauts gathered at Space Center Houston to share stories from their missions aboard the International Space Station and recognize the teamwork and people on the ground t…
articles.body: The Expedition 73 crew attends a debrief and awards ceremony at Space Center Houston’s IMAX theater on June 16, 2026. NASA/Luna Posadas Nava On June 16, astronauts and cosmonauts gathered at Space Center Houston to share stories from their missions aboard the International Space Station and recognize the teamwork and people on the ground that made their missions possible.  The Expedition 73 Welcome Home Ceremony brought together members of NASA’s SpaceX Crew-10, Soyuz MS-27, and NASA’s SpaceX Crew-11 missions.
articles.author: Sumer Loggins
